content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: How to Open a Case

  - url: https://help.etsy.com/hc/en-us/articles/5745586898199-How-to-Open-a-Case

  - source: Etsy Help Center

  - domain: help.etsy.com


  ## Content


  # How to Open a Case


  A buyer can ask Etsy to assist with an order after first contacting the seller and
  after the order meets Etsy''s case-opening conditions. For qualifying orders, Etsy
  may refund purchases that do not arrive, arrive damaged, or differ significantly
  from the listing description or photos.


  ## Opening a case


  To begin from Etsy.com, sign in, open **Purchases and reviews**, locate the relevant
  order, and select **Help with order**. Choose the issue that applies, provide supporting
  details, and select the available refund preference if Etsy presents one. The final
  case request is submitted to Etsy from the order-specific flow.


  An Etsy account is required. A guest purchase must be connected to an Etsy account
  before the buyer can use the case system.


  Etsy lists several case categories, including non-delivery, an item that is not
  as described, and an item that arrived damaged.


  ## When the Open a case option is not shown


  If **Open a case** is unavailable, Etsy may indicate that the request is too early,
  too late, or that a **Help with order** message must first be sent to the seller.
  A guest order that has not been linked to an Etsy account can also prevent access
  to the case flow.


  For physical items and digital items available for immediate download, both of these
  conditions must be satisfied:


  1. The estimated delivery date shown for the order at the time of purchase has passed.

  2. At least 48 hours have passed since the buyer sent the seller a **Help with order**
  request.


  For made-to-order digital items, the item must have been downloaded or at least
  seven days must have passed since purchase, and the 48-hour Help with order period
  must also have passed.


  When the request is still too early, Etsy may display the date and time when the
  buyer can start the case process in the **Still need help?** section of the Help
  with order request. Etsy also shows the remaining period for opening the case. The
  normal case window ends 30 days after the order''s estimated delivery date.


  ## What happens after a case is opened


  Etsy works with the buyer and seller through the case log and may ask for photographs,
  measurements, third-party evaluations, or other information needed to decide the
  case. Etsy communicates its final decision to both parties.


  A case may close automatically after a full refund or if the buyer disputes the
  charge with a card provider, bank, or PayPal. A charge dispute can prevent a buyer
  from opening an Etsy case because only one credit method can be used.


  Refund handling may differ when payment was processed through standalone PayPal
  or another third-party payment processor. Those providers have their own dispute
  rules.

  '

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: How to Get Help with An Order

  - url: https://help.etsy.com/hc/en-us/articles/4402660818583-How-to-Get-Help-with-An-Order

  - source: Etsy Help Center

  - domain: help.etsy.com


  ## Content


  # How to Get Help with An Order


  For a problem involving an Etsy purchase, the seller is normally the first person
  who can help. Sellers can answer questions about an order''s status, shipping address,
  requested changes, refunds, returns, exchanges, or cancellation.


  ## Send a Help with order request


  On Etsy.com:


  1. Sign in and open **Your account**.

  2. Go to **Purchases and reviews**.

  3. Find the relevant purchase and select **Help with Order**.

  4. Choose the type of help needed.

  5. Add a message, the requested resolution, and supporting images when the selected
  issue allows them.

  6. Select **Send help request**.


  In the Etsy app, open **You**, choose **Purchases**, select the order, and use the
  order''s help options to message the seller and submit the help request.


  The order-specific Help with order flow is more than a general message entry point.
  It creates the request that Etsy uses as part of the later case process.


  ## When the seller cannot resolve the issue


  If the seller cannot help, the buyer must use Etsy''s case system to request further
  assistance from Etsy. For qualifying purchases, Etsy''s Purchase Protection program
  may provide a full refund when an item does not arrive, arrives damaged, or is not
  as described.


  Etsy''s separate case-opening guidance explains when **Open a case** becomes available.
  Contacting the seller does not make the case option immediately available; the eligibility
  and waiting conditions for the order still apply.


  ## Guest orders


  A buyer who checked out as a guest must connect the purchase to an Etsy account
  before opening a case. Linking the order makes it available in the buyer''s purchase
  history and allows the order-specific help process to continue.

  '

content.content:
The correct next step is to **wait for Etsy’s required waiting period**, rather than use an alternative route.

For order **ETSY-2026-0711-4827**:

- The issue is recorded as **item arrived damaged**.
- You already submitted the required **Help with order** request to the seller.
- Etsy requires both the estimated delivery date to have passed and **48 hours after contacting the seller** before a case can be opened.
- Your request was submitted **July 11, 2026 at 12:30 AM PDT**.
- The 48-hour period ends **July 13, 2026 at 12:30 AM PDT**.
- It is currently **July 11, 2026 at 6:00 PM PDT**, so only 17.5 hours have elapsed.

The **Open a case** option is therefore unavailable because the request is still too early—not because of a technical problem. If the seller has not resolved the issue after the eligibility time, return to **Purchases and reviews → Help with order → Still need help?** and start the case from that order-specific flow. Keep photographs and other evidence of the damage ready.

- The 48-hour period ends **July 13, 2026 at 12:30 AM PDT**.

The **Open a case** option is therefore unavailable because the request is still too early—not because of a technical problem. If the seller has not resolved the issue after the eligibility time, return to **Purchases and reviews → Help with order → Still need help?** and start the case from that order-specific flow. Keep photographs and other evidence of the damage ready.
